question: Why does inspect-room 219 219 fail after the offline candidate ranking selects scene 219 as the top decoded interior scene?

author: codex
current_best_answer: The current same-index 219/219 pair fails because inspect-room now reports six misaligned_min fragment-zone issues before room admission; the first issue is zone 1 on the z axis (4208..5744), and additional GRM zones fail on z or x min alignment.
next_probe: Use the landed ranking plus fragment-zone compatibility diagnostics to determine whether any higher-ranked same-index decoded interior pair clears the current room-state bounds rules, or prove the current same-index strategy is a dead end.

current_best_answer: Yes. triage-same-index-decoded-interior-candidates reports 86/86 as the highest-ranked compatible same-index pair above baseline, but it is a zero-fragment/zero-GRM pass; 187/187 is the first fragment-bearing compatible pair above baseline.
next_probe: Decide whether the next Phase 5 slice should follow the highest-ranked compatible pair overall or the first fragment-bearing compatible pair, and keep zero-GRM passes distinct from fragment evidence.

author: codex
current_best_answer: Treat both opcodes as one-byte structural markers in the canonical offline decoder: LM_DEFAULT and LM_END_SWITCH no longer block real-asset decoding, and future work should move to runtime/gameplay semantics instead of marker-width proof.
next_probe: Use the widened Branch-A boundary to choose the next runtime/gameplay slice, and only reopen switch-family proof if a later semantic/runtime question requires it.
question: How should LM_DEFAULT and LM_END_SWITCH be handled for scene-surface life integration?

author: codex
current_best_answer: Treat the starts as differentiated, not generic: 19/19 currently favors dense_swapped_axes_64 as a materially better diagnostic-only mapping, 2/2 favors swapped_axes_512_control, and 11/10 shows no better alternative mapping.
next_probe: Choose the next runtime-facing slice from these differentiated hints instead of widening to another room/load pair by default.
